title: Pandas
setup:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import io

examples.code:
# A Series is a labelled 1-D array
s = pd.Series([1, 7, 2, 3])
print(s)
examples.result:
0    1
1    7
2    2
3    3
dtype: int64

examples.code:
# Custom index — works like a dict with order
calories = pd.Series(
    [420, 380, 390],
    index=["day1", "day2", "day3"]
)
print(calories)
print("Day 1:", calories["day1"])
examples.result:
day1    420
day2    380
day3    390
dtype: int64
Day 1: 420

examples.code:
# Key attributes
print("dtype :", s.dtype)
print("values:", s.values)
print("index :", s.index.tolist())
print("mean  :", s.mean())
examples.result:
dtype : int64
values: [1 7 2 3]
index : [0, 1, 2, 3]
mean  : 3.25

examples.code:
# A DataFrame is a 2-D table — like a spreadsheet
data = {
    "name":  ["Alice", "Bob", "Carol"],
    "score": [88, 92, 79],
    "grade": ["B", "A", "C"],
}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df)
examples.result:
name  score grade
0  Alice     88     B
1    Bob     92     A
2  Carol     79     C

examples.code:
# Column → Series;  row → via iloc
print(df["score"])
print()
print(df.iloc[1])
examples.result:
0    88
1    92
2    79
Name: score, dtype: int64

name     Bob
score     92
grade      A
Name: 1, dtype: object

examples.code:
# Filter rows; add a calculated column
high = df[df["score"] > 80]
print(high)
print()
df["passed"] = df["score"] >= 80
print(df)
examples.result:
name  score grade
0  Alice     88     B
1    Bob     92     A

    name  score grade  passed
0  Alice     88     B    True
1    Bob     92     A    True
2  Carol     79     C   False

examples.code:
# Real project: df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
# Pyodide: feed a string via io.StringIO
csv_data = """Duration,Pulse,Calories
60,110,409
60,117,479
45,103,340
45,109,282
60,102,300
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
print(df)

# Load only selected columns
df2 = pd.read_csv(
    io.StringIO(csv_data),
    usecols=["Duration", "Calories"]
)
print(df2)

# Real project: df = pd.read_json('data.json')
json_str = """[
  {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "city": "London"},
  {"name": "Bob",   "age": 30, "city": "Paris"},
  {"name": "Carol", "age": 22, "city": "Berlin"}
]"""
df = pd.read_json(io.StringIO(json_str))
print(df)
examples.result:
name  age    city
0  Alice   25  London
1    Bob   30   Paris
2  Carol   22  Berlin

examples.code:
# Column types and value counts
print(df.dtypes)
print()
print(df["city"].value_counts())
examples.result:
name    object
age      int64
city    object
dtype: object

city
London    1
Paris     1
Berlin    1
Name: count, dtype: int64

examples.code:
csv_data = """Duration,Pulse,Calories
60,110,409
60,117,479
45,103,340
45,109,282
60,102,300
60,112,415
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
print("head:")
print(df.head(3))
print("\ntail:")
print(df.tail(2))

head:

1        60    117       479

tail:

4        60    102       300
5        60    112       415

examples.code:
# Schema, types, and null counts
df.info()
examples.result:
<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>
RangeIndex: 6 entries, 0 to 5
Data columns (total 3 columns):
 #   Column    Non-Null Count  Dtype
---  ------    --------------  -----

examples.code:
# Full statistical summary
print(df.describe())

# Per-column aggregates
print("mean  :", df["Calories"].mean())
print("median:", df["Calories"].median())
print("max   :", df["Calories"].max())
print("min   :", df["Calories"].min())
print("std   :", df["Calories"].std().round(2))

# Inspect a dirty dataset
csv_data = """Duration,Pulse,Calories
60,110,409
60,,479
45,103,
,109,282
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
print(df)
print()
print("Missing values per column:")
print(df.isnull().sum())
examples.result:
Duration  Pulse  Calories
0      60.0  110.0     409.0
1      60.0    NaN     479.0
2      45.0  103.0       NaN
3       NaN  109.0     282.0

Missing values per column:
Duration    1
Pulse       1
Calories    1
dtype: int64

examples.code:
print("Shape:", df.shape)
print("Complete rows:", df.dropna().shape[0])
examples.result:
Shape: (4, 3)
Complete rows: 1

examples.code:
csv_data = """Duration,Pulse,Calories
60,110,409
60,,479
45,103,
,109,282
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

# Drop any row that contains at least one NaN
df_dropped = df.dropna()
print(df_dropped)
examples.result:
Duration  Pulse  Calories
0      60.0  110.0     409.0

examples.code:
# Fill every NaN with a fixed value
df_filled = df.fillna(0)
print(df_filled)

# Fill each column with its own mean (smarter)
df_smart = df.copy()
for col in df_smart.columns:
    df_smart[col] = df_smart[col].fillna(df_smart[col].mean())
print(df_smart.round(1))

# Fix inconsistent date strings → uniform datetime
csv_data = """Date,Duration
2020-12-01,60
2020/12/02,45
20201203,60
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
print("Before:", df["Date"].dtype)
df["Date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["Date"], format="mixed")
print("After :", df["Date"].dtype)
print(df)
examples.result:
Before: object
After : datetime64[us]
        Date  Duration
0 2020-12-01        60
1 2020-12-02        45
2 2020-12-03        60

examples.code:
# Strip stray quotes and convert to numeric
csv_data = """product,price
apple,"1.50"
banana,"0.75"
cherry,"2.00"
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
df["price"] = pd.to_numeric(df["price"].str.replace('"', ''))
print(df.dtypes)
print(df)
examples.result:
product    object
price     float64
dtype: object
  product  price
0   apple   1.50
1  banana   0.75
2  cherry   2.00

examples.code:
# Spot and fix obvious outliers
csv_data = """Duration,Pulse
450,110
60,117
45,103
60,-5
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
print("Before:")
print(df)

df.loc[df["Duration"] > 120, "Duration"] = 120
df.loc[df["Pulse"] < 40, "Pulse"] = np.nan
print("\nAfter:")
print(df)
examples.result:
Before:
   Duration  Pulse
0       450    110
1        60    117
2        45    103
3        60     -5

After:
   Duration  Pulse
0       120  110.0
1        60  117.0
2        45  103.0
3        60    NaN

examples.code:
# Replace impossible values with NaN using .where()
csv_data = """name,age
Alice,25
Bob,999
Carol,22
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
df["age"] = df["age"].where(df["age"] < 120, other=np.nan)
print(df)
examples.result:
name   age
0  Alice  25.0
1    Bob   NaN
2  Carol  22.0

examples.code:
csv_data = """name,score
Alice,88
Bob,92
Alice,88
Carol,79
Bob,92
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
print("Duplicated rows:")
print(df.duplicated())
print("\nTotal duplicates:", df.duplicated().sum())
examples.result:
Duplicated rows:
0    False
1    False
2     True
3    False
4     True
dtype: bool

Total duplicates: 2

examples.code:
# Remove duplicates — keeps first occurrence by default
df_unique = df.drop_duplicates()
print(df_unique)
print("Rows before:", len(df), "→ after:", len(df_unique))
examples.result:
name  score
0  Alice     88
1    Bob     92
3  Carol     79
Rows before: 5 → after: 3

examples.code:
# Deduplicate on a single column
df_by_name = df.drop_duplicates(subset=["name"])
print(df_by_name)
examples.result:
name  score
0  Alice     88
1    Bob     92
3  Carol     79

examples.code:
csv_data = """Duration,Calories,Pulse
60,409,110
60,479,117
45,340,103
45,282,109
60,300,102
60,410,112
30,190,98
45,350,108
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

# -1 = perfect negative, 0 = no relation, 1 = perfect positive
print(df.corr(numeric_only=True).round(2))
examples.result:
Duration  Calories  Pulse
Duration      1.00      0.80   0.66
Calories      0.80      1.00   0.88
Pulse         0.66      0.88   1.00

Duration ↔ Calories: 0.798
→ Strong correlation

examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

csv_data = """Duration,Calories
60,409
60,479
45,340
45,282
60,300
60,410
30,190
45,350
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

df.plot(
    kind="scatter",
    x="Duration",
    y="Calories",
    title="Duration vs Calories",
    color="steelblue"
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

csv_data = """Duration,Calories
60,409
60,479
45,340
45,282
60,300
60,410
30,190
45,350
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

df["Calories"].plot(
    kind="hist",
    bins=5,
    title="Calorie Distribution",
    color="coral",
    edgecolor="white"
)
plt.xlabel("Calories")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

scores = pd.Series(
    {"Alice": 88, "Bob": 92, "Carol": 79, "Dave": 85}
)
scores.plot(kind="bar", title="Student Scores", color="mediumseagreen")
plt.ylabel("Score")
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

csv_data = """week,sales
1,120
2,145
3,132
4,178
5,160
6,195
"""
df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

df.plot(
    x="week",
    y="sales",
    kind="line",
    title="Weekly Sales Trend",
    marker="o",
    color="mediumpurple"
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
